context: Tests whether the agent correctly understands the limitations of REGISTRY_ONLY (proxy-controlled, bypassable), identifies gaps in egress coverage (wildcard ServiceEntry, no network-level controls, S3 not routed through egress gateway), and recommends layered controls rather than treating REGISTRY_ONLY as a firewall.

checklist.name: REGISTRY_ONLY is not a firewall
checklist.description: Assessment explicitly states that REGISTRY_ONLY is NOT an egress firewall — it only blocks unknown destinations handled by the proxy and can be bypassed by traffic that escapes mesh capture (e.g. host-network pods, non-sidecar workloads)

checklist.name: Bypass paths identified
checklist.description: Report identifies at least one category of traffic that can bypass REGISTRY_ONLY (e.g. pods on host network, unenrolled workloads, direct IP connections outside proxy capture)

checklist.name: Wildcard ServiceEntry finding
checklist.description: Flags that the *.amazonaws.com ServiceEntry is overly broad and allows any AWS endpoint, not just the intended S3 bucket(s)

checklist.name: S3 not egress-gateway-routed
checklist.description: Identifies that the *.amazonaws.com ServiceEntry has no corresponding VirtualService routing through the egress gateway — meaning S3 traffic is not actually inspected/routed through the gateway even though it is registered

checklist.name: No network-level controls finding
checklist.description: Flags the absence of Kubernetes NetworkPolicy or infrastructure-level network controls as a gap, since without them direct-IP traffic can escape mesh capture entirely

checklist.name: Layered control recommendation
checklist.description: Remediation recommends pairing REGISTRY_ONLY with network-level controls (e.g. Kubernetes NetworkPolicy or cloud firewall) that block direct outbound connections independently of the proxy

checklist.name: Scoped ServiceEntry recommendation
checklist.description: Recommends replacing wildcard ServiceEntry with scoped entries limited to specific hosts or CIDR ranges

checklist.name: VERIFIED/INFERRED/UNKNOWN labels
checklist.description: Each finding uses one of VERIFIED, INFERRED, or UNKNOWN to describe its evidence status

checklist.name: Attack scenario present
checklist.description: At least one finding includes a concrete attack scenario describing source, hops, and target (e.g. compromised workload with host-network access exfiltrating data bypassing proxy)

checklist.name: Validation steps present
checklist.description: At least one finding includes validation steps that specify both a positive test (allowed egress succeeds) and a negative test (blocked egress fails)

checklist.name: Remediation time horizons
checklist.description: Remediation plan contains at least Immediate and 30-day sections, not a flat undifferentiated list
